title: Normalized InboundMessage
description: The normalized, runtime-agnostic message the orchestrator (orchestrator.js #buildInbound) hands to InboundDelivery.deliver(msg, endpoint, priority) AFTER dedupe -> detail-fetch -> field-hoist -> conversation-fetch -> access-policy. `endpoint` and `priority` are also passed as separate positional args but are mirrored here. `additionalProperties:false` is deliberate: it makes this schema a drift alarm — if the SDK starts emitting a new top-level field it must be added here first.

orgSlug.description: DEPRECATED alias of orgId (== orgConfig.org_id); kept for consumers still reading orgSlug

orgName.description: orgConfig.org_name (omitted when the config has none).

conversation.description: The resolved conversation record ({id, type, name, ...}) or a minimal {id} fallback when the fetch failed.

conversationType.description: Lowercased conv.type, else derived ('thread' if thread_id present, else 'dm'). Known values: dm | group | thread. Kept as a free string because the value is server-driven (conv.type).

seq.description: Conversation sequence number (omitted when the message carries none).

senderId.description: Sender member id (msg.sender_id, hoisted from message.sender_id). OPTIONAL: when message-detail cannot resolve the sender the SDK still delivers the message and this field is absent — so it is intentionally NOT in `required`. Present in the common case.

senderType.description: HUMAN | AGENT | SYSTEM. May be '' when the frame/detail omit it.

type.description: Lowercased message content type (e.g. 'text'). May be '' when absent.

text.description: Extracted body text (content.body.text | string content), '' when none.

endpoint.description: formatEndpoint() routing key: <conversationId>[|reply:..][|thread:..][|parent:..]. Also passed as the 2nd positional arg to deliver().

priority.description: systemEventPriority(msg): 1|2|3, or omitted (undefined) when the message carries no metadata.systemEvent. Also passed as the 3rd positional arg to deliver().

via.description: Arrival path: live WS frame ('ws') or /sync catch-up ('sync').

message.description: The full merged raw frame+detail (notification spread over fetched detail). Opaque pass-through so the adapter can do media/history/quoted-message/C4-envelope work without re-fetching.

Priority.title: DeliveryPriority
Priority.description: Neutral 1=urgent / 2=high / 3=normal scale (protocol/system-message.js). Adapters map it to their own delivery-priority mechanism.

Decision.title: AccessDecision (handle:true form)
Decision.description: The access-policy decision (protocol/access-policy.js decideInbound). Only the handle:true variant reaches a delivered InboundMessage — drops (handle:false) never call deliver(). Extra hint fields (bindOwnerHint / ownerNameHint / mode / mentioned / groupCfg) appear depending on the branch.

mode.description: Group mode (group/thread branch only). 'smart' = handle every message in the allowlisted group without requiring an @-mention. 'mention' = only handle when the agent is @-mentioned; non-mention traffic is dropped (handle:false) and never delivered. 'silent' = OBSERVE-ONLY: the message IS delivered to the agent (handle:true) so it can update per-conversation context (participant/@mention registry, group history) and nothing is lost, but the adapter MUST NOT auto-reply to a silent-mode delivery. All four *-openmax adapters treat 'silent' as deliver-but-do-not-respond (the zylos 静默生命周期缓冲 analog).

ownerNameHint.description: Owner display name to persist (DM owner-exempt first-name-fill).
